questions.question: According to the lesson, how should 'success' be defined in the context of facing fears?

options.text: Feeling zero anxiety during an exposure exercise
Trying, learning, and showing up -- regardless of the level of anxiety experienced
Completing your entire Fear Ladder in one week
Never feeling the urge to avoid a situation again

questions.explanation: Success is redefined as trying (making the attempt), learning (staying long enough for your brain to gather new data), and showing up (continuing the journey). It is NOT about feeling zero anxiety -- if you wait until you are not scared, you might wait forever.

questions.question: What does the lesson mean by 'Bravery is a muscle, not a feeling'?

options.text: You need to exercise regularly to reduce anxiety
You do not wait until you feel brave to act -- you act, and that builds the feeling of bravery over time
Physical strength is required to face your fears
Bravery is something you are born with and cannot develop

questions.explanation: Bravery is not a feeling you wait for before acting. It is built through action. Every time you choose to face a fear despite feeling anxious, you are building the 'bravery muscle.' Like any muscle, it gets stronger with repeated use.

questions.question: What is the key difference between avoidance and gentle approaching?

options.text: Avoidance requires more effort than approaching
Avoidance shrinks your world over time, while approaching gradually expands it
Approaching eliminates anxiety permanently, while avoidance only reduces it temporarily
There is no meaningful difference -- both strategies manage anxiety equally well

questions.explanation: Avoidance provides temporary relief but shrinks your world by reinforcing fear and limiting your activities. Gentle approaching (exposure) gradually expands your world by providing your brain with new evidence that you can handle uncomfortable situations.

questions.question: Even when you cannot control the feeling of anxiety, what CAN you control?

options.text: Other people's reactions to your anxiety
Your actions -- you can choose to stay, breathe, and use your tools even when it is hard
How long the anxious feeling lasts
Whether anxiety shows up in the first place

questions.explanation: This is one of the most empowering lessons: even when you cannot control the feeling of anxiety, you can still control your actions. You can choose to stay in the situation, use your breathing skills, engage your grounding tools, and ride the wave -- even when it is hard.

questions.question: What should you do if you experience setbacks or difficult days while working through your Fear Ladder?

options.text: Conclude that the approach is not working and stop
Be patient and compassionate with yourself -- progress is not a straight line, and a difficult day does not erase your gains
Jump to a higher rung to push through the setback
Start your entire ladder over from the beginning

questions.explanation: Progress is not a straight line. Some days will be easier than others, and a difficult day does not erase the progress you have made. The lesson encourages patience, self-compassion, and celebrating every small step as a move toward freedom.

title: You Are Brave: Celebrating Your Courage - Quiz
